resources.name: VirusTotal
resources.best_use: Multi-engine reputation for a hash, URL, domain, or IP, plus related-sample and relationship pivots.
resources.caveats: Hash/URL/IP lookups are non-content-disclosing, but the query itself reveals the indicator to the vendor. Uploading a FILE shares it with the VirusTotal community (downloadable by paid/enterprise users), so do not upload confidential samples without authorization.

resources.name: Team Cymru Malware Hash Registry
resources.best_use: Keyless hash reputation. A DNS TXT lookup of <md5-or-sha1>.malware.hash.cymru.com returns the last-seen time and an antivirus detection percentage when the hash is known. SHA-256 is supported over the HTTPS interface.
resources.caveats: No key, agent-friendly. MD5 and SHA-1 work over the DNS interface. A SHA-256 is too long for a DNS label, so use the HTTPS interface for SHA-256 (get_file_info and analyze_file report both MD5 and SHA-256). Returns NXDOMAIN for unknown hashes. Non-commercial use only, do not enumerate, and notify Team Cymru before high-volume or automated queries. A non-zero detection percentage is a lead to corroborate, not a verdict.

resources.name: MalwareBazaar
resources.best_use: Is this hash known, and what related samples exist (by SHA256, imphash, TLSH, tag, or family).
resources.caveats: A public malware sample exchange. Hash lookups are non-content-disclosing, but the query reveals the hash to abuse.ch. Samples you upload are shared with the community. A free abuse.ch Auth-Key is required for API access.

resources.name: Hybrid Analysis
resources.best_use: Behavioral sandbox reports and reputation for a hash or URL; search existing reports before submitting.
resources.caveats: Public submissions are searchable by others. Choose a non-public submission setting where available. A free account is required for full access.

resources.name: Intezer Analyze
resources.best_use: Code-reuse and genetic similarity to known malware families; anchors corroboration on sample-intrinsic code, not network IOCs.
resources.caveats: Free tier has a limited number of scans. Uploaded files are shared with Intezer.
resources.stability: Established commercial service with a persistent free tier.

resources.name: ANY.RUN
resources.best_use: Interactive cloud sandbox with real-time observation; also search existing public submissions by hash or IOC.
resources.caveats: Free-tier interactive tasks are PUBLIC. Do not detonate confidential samples on the free tier.
resources.stability: Widely used interactive sandbox, stable free tier.

resources.name: Joe Sandbox Cloud Basic
resources.best_use: Detailed behavioral reports for a submitted sample or URL.
resources.caveats: Cloud Basic submissions are public. Use a paid or private tier for sensitive samples.
resources.stability: Established sandbox vendor with a free Basic tier.

resources.name: Recorded Future Triage
resources.best_use: Sandbox detonation with configuration extraction; search existing public reports by hash.
resources.caveats: Free research accounts default to public samples. Mark submissions unlisted or private for sensitive material.

resources.name: CAPE Sandbox
resources.best_use: Detonation with payload and configuration extraction for many families.
resources.caveats: The community instance is public; submissions, configs, and results are visible. Run a private CAPE for sensitive work.

resources.name: Malpedia
resources.best_use: Normalize family names and aliases across vendors; family references, descriptions, and YARA.
resources.caveats: A reference resource, not a submission engine. Some samples and non-public YARA require an account or invite.

resources.name: YARAify
resources.best_use: Match a file or hash against public YARA and ClamAV rules when vendor naming is weak.
resources.caveats: Scanning a file submits it. A free abuse.ch Auth-Key is required for API access.

resources.name: ThreatFox
resources.best_use: Pivot from a malware family to its current C2 and payload infrastructure, and vice versa.
resources.caveats: Community-submitted IOCs are leads, not ground truth, and can be poisoned. Corroborate. A free abuse.ch Auth-Key is required for API access.

resources.name: urlscan.io
resources.best_use: Live URL and page analysis (screenshot, DOM, JavaScript, request chain) and search of existing public scans by domain, IP, ASN, or hash.
resources.caveats: The keyless path is searching existing public scans; submitting a new scan needs an account. Public scans are searchable by anyone, so use unlisted or private visibility for sensitive URLs. Submitting an attacker URL fetches their infrastructure and may alert them.

resources.name: URLhaus
resources.best_use: Look up a malware-distribution URL, host, or IP, and pivot to related payloads.
resources.caveats: A free abuse.ch Auth-Key is required for API access.

resources.name: Sucuri SiteCheck
resources.best_use: Live remote scan of a site for malware and blocklist status.
resources.caveats: The scan fetches the URL live from Sucuri infrastructure (not yours). It does not connect from your environment, but it still touches attacker infrastructure, which can tip off an adversary watching their server, so avoid it for sensitive live URLs.

resources.name: Google Safe Browsing
resources.best_use: Check a site's current Safe Browsing status.
resources.caveats: Status lookup only. The Safe Browsing API (for automation) requires a key.

resources.name: GreyNoise
resources.best_use: Decide whether an IP is opportunistic internet background noise or worth deeper investigation.
resources.caveats: The free community lookup works without an API key and classifies an IP as noise or not. Richer data and higher limits are paid.
resources.stability: Established, widely used for IP triage.

resources.name: Shodan InternetDB
resources.best_use: Fast IP lookup for open ports, hostnames, CPEs, tags, and known vulnerabilities.
resources.caveats: Unauthenticated and free for non-commercial use. Full Shodan (banners, history, search) needs an account and API key.

resources.name: Censys Search
resources.best_use: Map exposed infrastructure and pivot on hosts, services, banners, and certificates.
resources.caveats: Free tier requires an account and is rate-limited.

resources.name: AbuseIPDB
resources.best_use: Community reputation and abuse reports for an IP or hostname.
resources.caveats: Community reports are leads, not ground truth. A free account and API key are needed for the API.

resources.name: Cisco Talos Reputation Center
resources.best_use: Independent IP, domain, and email reputation cross-check, useful for network and email infrastructure.
resources.caveats: Reputation snapshot; corroborate with other sources.

resources.name: ipinfo
resources.best_use: Geolocation, ASN, and owner baseline for an IP.
resources.caveats: Free tier with an account is rate-limited; richer data is paid.

resources.name: crt.sh
resources.best_use: Certificate Transparency search to pivot on domains, organization names, and certificate fingerprints.
resources.caveats: Passive lookup against public CT logs. The service can be slow or briefly unavailable under load, so retry or fall back to another CT source if it times out.

resources.name: DNSDumpster
resources.best_use: Passive DNS, subdomain, and host discovery for a domain.
resources.caveats: Free and rate-limited.

resources.name: SecurityTrails
resources.best_use: Passive DNS, DNS history, subdomain discovery, and domain-to-IP infrastructure pivots.
resources.caveats: Free tier with an account; paid for volume.

resources.name: Validin
resources.best_use: DNS and threat-infrastructure enrichment, mapping artifacts to malware-family and attacker profiles.
resources.caveats: Free community tier with an account.
resources.stability: Newer but actively maintained; reassess at re-verification.

resources.name: CyberGordon
resources.best_use: Run one observable across many reputation services at once for a fast first corroboration pass.
resources.caveats: Aggregates third-party services, so each underlying source carries its own caveats.

resources.name: Pulsedive
resources.best_use: Enrich an IP, domain, URL, or hash from OSINT feeds, with passive and active scan options.
resources.caveats: Free tier with an account. Treat feed-sourced data as leads.

resources.name: IBM X-Force Exchange
resources.best_use: Independent reputation and context for an IP, URL, or hash to corroborate other sources.
resources.caveats: A free account is required.

resources.name: AlienVault OTX
resources.best_use: Check whether an IOC appears in community pulses and campaign context.
resources.caveats: Lead generation, not ground truth. Community pulses can be poisoned; corroborate. Now operated as LevelBlue OTX. A free account is required.

resources.name: OpenPhish
resources.best_use: Check a URL or domain against a phishing feed.
resources.caveats: Free for non-commercial use.

resources.name: DShield (SANS ISC)
resources.best_use: Check an IP against suspicious-source blocklists and attack telemetry from the SANS Internet Storm Center.
resources.caveats: Aggregated telemetry; read the list methodology before acting.

resources.name: UnpacMe
resources.best_use: Automated unpacking and payload or configuration extraction when triage stalls on a packed sample.
resources.caveats: Not pure OSINT; submission is disclosing. Free-tier results are public.

resources.name: RDAP (rdap.org)
resources.best_use: Registration data (RDAP, the JSON successor to WHOIS) for a domain or IP via a single no-key request, for registrar, network-owner, and allocation pivots.
resources.caveats: rdap.org redirects to the authoritative registry or RIR and returns clean JSON with no key, so it is agent-friendly. Domain coverage depends on the TLD supporting RDAP.
resources.stability: IETF-standard protocol with a stable bootstrap redirector.

resources.name: Silent Push Community Edition
resources.best_use: Infrastructure hunting across DNS, WHOIS, certificates, and web-content fingerprints to map related attacker infrastructure.
resources.caveats: The free Community Edition allows 250 queries per month (GUI and API) for DNS, IP, domain, and WHOIS-history lookups. Advanced features and intelligence feeds are paid.
resources.stability: Established platform with a free Community Edition.

resources.name: Maltego Community Edition
resources.best_use: Link-analysis and graph pivoting across many data sources via transforms.
resources.caveats: The free Community Edition (requires a Maltego ID) includes about 50 transforms, 200 credits per month, and up to 24 results per transform, with per-provider monthly caps. Breach data and higher limits are paid.
resources.stability: Long-established link-analysis platform with a free Community Edition.
